vulnerability.reference: Lack of diverse adversarial examples for all demographic groups during training amplifies per-class robustness gaps.
vulnerability.generated: Image recognition models lack adversarial robustness to fairness-targeted perturbations, allowing small input changes to cause large group-wise disparities.

impact.reference: Disproportionate misclassification of minority groups in autonomous driving leads to higher collision rates and public safety risks.
impact.generated: Disproportionate misclassification across demographic groups results in biased autonomous driving decisions and unequal safety outcomes for end-users.

dose_response.reference: Crafting adversarial perturbations to compromise fairness amplifies per-class robustness gaps due to limited diverse examples, causing autonomous driving systems to misclassify minority pedestrians, raising collision risks.
dose_response.generated: Adversarial fairness perturbations causing disproportionate misclassification, through exposure to end-users of autonomous driving perception, trigger models' insufficient adversarial robustness to fairness-targeted perturbations, leading to biased autonomous driving decisions and unequal safety outcomes.
